global.title: Reactor Sync Infrastructure

coreIdeas.contextPhrase: Operation log is the substrate — every sync primitive here carries ordered ops, not state snapshots.
IChannel mailboxes define how channels hand ops to the reactor — the shape SwarmChannel and GQLChannel both fit.
Three-table sync persistence model — precursor view of SyncManager state.
Four-table variant that includes the mailbox buffer — the current-as-of-today persistence layout.
Multi-remote is the lever that lets Swarm and GQL sync the same collection simultaneously.
Shows how SwarmChannel maps push/pull onto the reactor's ordinal batches and cursors.
Dual-storage native pattern (PGlite + Postgres) — the baseline the Swarm mirror extends.

global.description: The reactor-side plumbing that makes decentralized channels possible: operation log, IChannel mailboxes, sync tables, and multi-remote support.
global.orientation: This MOC captures the reactor sync primitives that every transport — GraphQL or Swarm — composes with. Operations-as-append-only-log is the substrate; IChannel mailboxes (inbox/outbox/deadLetter) are the per-remote seam; SyncManager persists state in PGlite tables (remotes, cursors, dead letters, and the mailbox buffer); and multi-remote-per-collection is the switch that enables the dual-sync strategy. These notes form the backbone needed to reason about the SwarmChannel, debouncing, and recovery.
